preparations.preparation_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum is a homeopathic remedy derived from the horse chestnut tree, commonly used to treat symptoms of varicose veins and hemorrhoids. The tablet is often taken to reduce inflammation, swelling, and discomfort associated with these conditions. Homeopaths recommend using Aesculus hippocastanum in conjunction with other treatments for optimal relief.

Aesculus hippocastanum is an extract derived from the bark of the horse chestnut tree, commonly used in skincare products to alleviate symptoms of varicose veins and spider veins. This extract is often incorporated into lotions and creams to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. By incorporating aesculus hippocastanum lotion into one's daily skincare routine, individuals may experience a noticeable reduction in the appearance of unsightly veins.

Aesculus hippocastanum is a deciduous tree commonly known as the horse chestnut, and its capsule is a spiky, green fruit that splits open to reveal shiny, red-brown seeds. The capsule is a key feature of the tree, attracting birds and small mammals that help disperse the seeds. As the seeds mature, the capsule dries and splits open, releasing the seeds to the ground.
